title: Pillowy Soft Polish Pork Dumplings
description: Savory Polish dish featuring vanilla bean paste and sous-vide cooking, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this recipe is low in fat and rich in flavor. The dumplings are pillowy soft and full of tender pork.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, vanilla bean paste, chopped onion, and minced garlic.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, egg, salt, and black pepper.
Add the dry ingredients to the pork mixture and mix until just combined.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Divide the mixture into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Place the dumplings in a single layer in a sous-vide bag and add the chicken broth.
Seal the bag and cook in a water bath at 180 degrees Fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Remove the dumplings from the bag and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
